about.about.4: One of East and Central Africa's fastest growing energy trading and transportation conglomerates, founded 2006.
about.about.6: From a Single Fuel Company to a Regional Powerhouse
about.about.7: Lake Group was established by its founding CEO and Chairman Mr. Ally Edha Awadh in 2006 with the opening of its flagship company, Lake Oil: a humble beginning in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania.
about.about.9: Since inception, the growth story of Lake Group has been vertical, becoming one of the fastest-growing energy trading, logistics and construction supply material conglomerates in East and Central Africa and the Middle East.
about.about.10: Today, Lake Group distributes petroleum products in 8 countries, owns storage facilities across the region, manufactures lubricants and ready-mix concrete, and operates a fleet of more than 700 trucks.
about.about.12: With a diverse mix of 21 nationalities and a total workforce of 4,600+ employees, Lake Group continues to grow with purpose.
about.about.23: To provide customers with quality products and services in a safe, efficient and cost-effective manner without damaging the environment, identifying and entering new areas of business while creating a work environment where employees exceed their personal best.
about.about.24: Guided by our core values: Teamwork, Reliability, Integrity and Customer Satisfaction.
about.about.29: To become the leading regional convenience retailer and marketer of products and services while achieving continuous improvement through operational excellence, innovation and a culture based on performance.
about.about.41: Founded Lake Group at age 27 in 2006
about.about.44: "Welcome to a diverse pioneer in fuel marketing, haulage, LPG bottling, cylinder manufacturing and concrete production. With our experienced team across all business units, we are fully geared to meet the demands of the global marketplace."
about.about.45: Under Mr. Awadh's leadership, Lake Group has successfully established itself in Oil & Gas, Engineering, Industrial Supplies, Projects, Cargo, Distribution, General Trading, Storage, Concrete Solutions and the Construction Sector, building a high-credit-rated organisation over nearly two decades.

concrete.concrete.4: Gulf Concrete and Cement Products, Dar es Salaam's market-leading ready-mix concrete supplier since 2010.
concrete.concrete.7: Gulf Concrete and Cement Products Company Ltd (GCCP), established in 2010, is one of the leading suppliers of ready-mix concrete in Dar es Salaam, involved in many of the city's most prestigious construction projects.
concrete.concrete.8: GCCP operates its own quarry at Lugoba with two aggregate plants of 250 tons/hour capacity each, producing 30,000 m³ of aggregate monthly. Its ready-mix operations are powered by Sany International batching plants from China.
concrete.concrete.9: In Dubai, Lake Group operates MERM (Middle East Ready Mix LLC), one of the largest premix plants in the region.
concrete.concrete.30: For projects requiring fast formwork removal and accelerated construction schedules.
concrete.concrete.32: Low shrinkage formulation for slabs, pavements and structures where crack control is critical.
concrete.concrete.34: Flows and consolidates under its own weight, ideal for congested reinforcement.
concrete.concrete.36: High-finish concrete for facades, decorative surfaces and exposed structural elements.
concrete.concrete.38: Permeable concrete for sustainable drainage in car parks, roads and landscaping.
concrete.concrete.40: High-strength, highly durable concrete for piling, deep foundations and critical structures.

container_services.container_services.4: African Inland Container Depot (AFICD) and Container Freight Station (ACFS), port extension solutions across East Africa.
container_services.container_services.6: The Inland Gateway to East African Trade
container_services.container_services.7: AFICD (African Inland Container Depot) and ACFS (African Container Freight Station) are Lake Group's port logistics arms, providing critical container storage, handling and customs services that extend port capacity into the hinterland.
container_services.container_services.8: With operations in Tanzania, Zambia and Mozambique, our container services are positioned at strategic corridors to serve regional importers, exporters and shipping lines.
container_services.container_services.21: Dar es Salaam, gateway to East Africa's largest economy

csr.csr.4: Lake Group is committed to giving back to the communities it serves and operating with minimal environmental impact.
csr.csr.7: As one of East Africa's largest private employers, Lake Group takes its social and environmental responsibilities seriously. The Group regularly provides support to community development through many of its CSR activities and is one of the leaders among its peers.
csr.csr.8: We believe that business growth and community development go hand in hand, and that our success must translate into tangible benefits for the people and environments in which we operate.
csr.csr.19: Supporting scholarships, vocational training and STEM education for youth in Tanzania and across the region, building the next generation of energy professionals.
csr.csr.21: Contributing to the construction and renovation of community facilities, roads, water points, health clinics and public amenities in operational areas.
csr.csr.23: Responsible handling of petroleum products, zero-tolerance for spillage, and progressive adoption of cleaner operations. Active reclamation of quarry land at Lugoba.
csr.csr.25: 4,600+ jobs across 8 countries, with a strong preference for hiring and developing local talent at every level of the organisation.
csr.csr.27: Supporting reliable LPG access for households across East Africa, reducing dependence on charcoal, improving air quality and lowering deforestation pressure.
csr.csr.29: Industry-leading safety standards across all divisions. Zero-harm ambition for employees, contractors and the communities we operate in.

fleet.fleet.4: Over 700 purpose-built trucks operating 24/7 across East and Central Africa, the backbone of our logistics operations.
fleet.fleet.13: Heavy-duty tankers for bulk liquid petroleum transport. Capacity from 30,000 to 60,000 litres. Full hazmat certification and GPS tracking.
fleet.fleet.18: Versatile flatbed and curtain-side trailers for steel, construction materials, containers and oversized cargo across the region.
fleet.fleet.22: Specialised pressurised tankers for bulk LPG transport between bottling plants and distribution points across East Africa.
fleet.fleet.26: 20 truck mixers (12m³ each) dedicated to GCCP ready-mix concrete delivery to construction sites across Dar es Salaam.
fleet.fleet.30: Reach stackers, terminal tractors and container trucks for AFICD inland depot operations in Tanzania, Zambia and Mozambique.
fleet.fleet.34: Support fleet of pickups, service vehicles and supervisor cars for field operations, maintenance and last-mile delivery support.

fuel.fuel.8: We supply white petroleum products including petrol, diesel and aviation fuel through retail stations, bulk delivery and strategic storage facilities across the region.

history.history.4: From a single fuel station to a pan-African conglomerate, the journey of Lake Group since 2006.
history.history.7: A story of bold decisions, relentless expansion, and building an energy empire from the ground up.
history.history.9: Ally Edha Awadh founds Lake Oil Ltd. at age 27 in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania, the seed of what would become Lake Group. Begins petroleum distribution with a small fleet.
history.history.11: Lake Trans Ltd. is established to handle bulk liquid haulage, rapidly expanding the Group's logistics footprint across Tanzania.
history.history.13: Gulf Concrete and Cement Products Company (GCCP) is established, quickly becoming the market leader in ready-mix concrete supply in Dar es Salaam.
history.history.15: Lake Group expands operations into Kenya, Zambia, Burundi and Rwanda, bringing fuel, LPG and lubricants to neighbouring markets.
history.history.17: Lake Steel Ltd. becomes the first company in Tanzania to introduce High Strength, Corrosion Resistant (HS-CR) reinforcement steel bars. A fully computerised rolling mill with 100,000 MT/yr capacity.
history.history.19: DRC Petroleum Ltd. and Wadi Elsundus Petroleum Co. (Ethiopia) are established, bringing the Group's operational footprint to 7 countries.
history.history.21: AFICD (African Inland Container Depot) is launched in Tanzania and Zambia. MERM (Middle East Ready Mix LLC) is established in Dubai, marking the Group's first presence outside Africa.
history.history.23: Lake Group surpasses 4,000 employees across 20+ subsidiaries, with 21 nationalities represented in the workforce. Mozambique operations begin.
history.history.25: Lake Gas launches innovative composite cylinders in Dar es Salaam, non-explosive, non-corrosive, lightweight and translucent, a revolution for East Africa's cooking fuel market.
history.history.28: Lake Group continues to expand, deepening its regional footprint, investing in new technologies, and driving economic development across East and Central Africa.
history.history.29: Be Part of the Next Chapter
history.history.30: Join our team or partner with us across Africa.

index.index.20: Every litre Lake Group delivers follows the same journey: refined petroleum flowing through our network to storage terminals and fuel stations across East and Central Africa.
index.index.32: A Conglomerate Built on Grit and Vision
index.index.33: Founded by CEO & Chairman Ally Edha Awadh in 2006 at just 27 years old, Lake Group has grown from a single fuel outlet into one of East and Central Africa's largest energy, logistics and industrial conglomerates.
index.index.35: Today, with 4,600+ employees across 21 nationalities, 700+ trucks, 85+ fuel stations and 20+ subsidiaries, Lake Group powers everyday life across Tanzania, Kenya, Zambia, Rwanda, Burundi, DRC, Ethiopia and Mozambique.
index.index.46: From fuel stations to steel mills, Lake Group operates across the full energy and industrial value chain of East Africa.
index.index.48: Top 5 petroleum distributor in Tanzania. Retail stations, bulk supply and storage across 8 countries.
index.index.50: Lake Gas operates LPG bottling and distribution with cylinders for domestic and commercial use across 7 countries.
index.index.53: Lake Trans operates 700+ trucks for bulk liquid haulage and regional logistics across East & Central Africa.
index.index.55: GCCP, Dar es Salaam's leading ready-mix concrete supplier. 30,000 m³ aggregate monthly from our Lugoba quarry.
index.index.57: Lake Lubes manufactures and distributes high-quality automotive, industrial and marine lubricants in 5 countries.
index.index.60: Tanzania's first HS-CR rebar producer. Fully automated rolling mill, 100,000 MT/year capacity.
index.index.62: AFICD & ACFS provide inland container depot and freight station services across TZ, ZM and MZ.
index.index.64: Operations across Tanzania, Kenya, Zambia, Rwanda, Burundi, DRC, Ethiopia, Mozambique & Dubai.
index.index.71: "Welcome to a diverse pioneer in fuel marketing, haulage, LPG bottling, cylinder manufacturing and concrete production. With experienced engineers and business professionals across our divisions, we are fully geared to meet the demands of the global marketplace."
index.index.73: CEO & Chairman, Lake Group  |  Founded Lake Group at age 27 in 2006

investors.investors.4: Financial highlights, company growth profile and governance information for current and prospective investors.
investors.investors.11: A Track Record of Vertical Growth
investors.investors.15: Lake Group has demonstrated consistent and significant growth since its founding in 2006. From a single petroleum distributor in Dar es Salaam, it has grown into a diversified conglomerate operating across 8 countries with 20+ subsidiaries.
investors.investors.16: Our financial strength, high credit rating in the global business community, and diversified revenue streams across energy, logistics and construction materials make Lake Group a resilient and high-potential investment.
investors.investors.17: The Group is privately held, headquartered in Tanzania, and has a direct economic impact in every market it operates, supporting local employment, supply chains and infrastructure development.
investors.investors.28: Eight distinct business lines across energy, logistics, manufacturing and construction reduce single-sector risk and provide stable multi-stream revenue.
investors.investors.30: Operations across 8 high-growth East and Central African markets, some of the world's fastest-growing economies by GDP and population.
investors.investors.32: From 1 company in 2006 to 20+ subsidiaries, a track record of disciplined expansion and value creation over 18 years.
investors.investors.34: Own storage facilities, truck fleet (700+), rolling mill, concrete plants, quarry and container depots, significant hard-asset base.
investors.investors.36: Founded and led by Ally Edha Awadh: an experienced entrepreneur who has built a high-credit-rated organisation over nearly two decades.
investors.investors.38: Active corporate social responsibility across all markets, building brand equity, local goodwill and sustainable business relationships.
investors.investors.39: Interested in Investing or Partnering with Lake Group?
investors.investors.40: Contact our corporate team for further information on the Group's financial profile and partnership opportunities.

leadership.leadership.4: The visionaries and industry professionals driving Lake Group's growth across East and Central Africa.
leadership.leadership.6: Guided by Experience. Driven by Vision.
leadership.leadership.7: Our leadership team brings together decades of expertise across energy, logistics, finance and engineering.
leadership.leadership.10: Founder of Lake Group (2006) · Forbes-recognised entrepreneur · Young Business Leader of the Year, African Leadership Magazine 2022
leadership.leadership.11: Mr. Awadh established Lake Oil at age 27 and grew it into one of East and Central Africa's largest energy, logistics and industrial conglomerates, operating across Tanzania, Kenya, Zambia, DRC, Burundi, Rwanda, Ethiopia and Mozambique with 4,600+ employees from 21 nationalities.
leadership.leadership.12: "With a team of experienced engineers and business professionals across our units, Lake Group is fully geared to meet the demands of the global marketplace, from fuel marketing and haulage to LPG, steel, lubricants and concrete."
leadership.leadership.13: Under his leadership the Group has expanded into Lake Trans, Lake Gas, Lake Lubes, Lake Steel, Gulf Cement & Concrete Products, African Inland Container Depot and regional oil storage and retail networks.
leadership.leadership.16: The executives overseeing Lake Group's day-to-day operations, people and strategic growth.
leadership.leadership.19: Leads Lake Oil's petroleum distribution, retail network and corporate partnerships, representing the flagship company at major industry and community events across Tanzania.
leadership.leadership.22: Oversees Group-wide executive operations and represents Lake Group at CSR initiatives, hospital donations and community outreach programmes across Dar es Salaam.
leadership.leadership.25: Heads people strategy and workforce development for Lake Oil, supporting the Group's diverse team of 4,600+ professionals across East and Central Africa.
leadership.leadership.28: Manages major Group projects including Lake Agro's greenfield development and coordinates humanitarian relief efforts such as flood-response aid in Rufiji and coastal regions.
leadership.leadership.31: Each subsidiary is led by sector specialists in oil & gas, logistics, engineering, manufacturing and port services.
leadership.leadership.33: Flagship petroleum importer, depot operator and retail network, Tanzania's top fuel distributors. MD: Abdulrahman Mohamed
leadership.leadership.35: 700+ truck logistics fleet for petroleum, dry cargo and cross-border haulage across East Africa.
leadership.leadership.37: LPG bottling, composite cylinders and East Africa's largest LPG storage terminal in Tanga.
leadership.leadership.39: Lubricant blending plant in Dar es Salaam producing greases and engine oils for regional markets.
leadership.leadership.41: Steel manufacturing including HS-CR rebars and construction materials for infrastructure projects.
leadership.leadership.43: Ready-mix concrete and aggregates, market leader in Dar es Salaam premix supply.
leadership.leadership.45: ICD, CFS and empty container depot services with rail-linked logistics in Tanzania.
leadership.leadership.47: Agricultural and greenfield project development. Projects Manager: Nassoro Abubakari
leadership.leadership.49: Lake Group's workforce reflects the rich diversity of the African continent, with 4,600+ professionals from 21 nationalities working together toward shared goals.

logistics.logistics.4: 700+ truck fleet providing bulk liquid transport and regional cargo haulage across East and Central Africa.
logistics.logistics.6: The Backbone of East Africa's Supply Chain
logistics.logistics.7: Lake Trans Ltd. is one of East Africa's largest logistics operators, running a fleet of 700+ trucks for bulk liquid haulage, general cargo and regional cross-border transport.
logistics.logistics.8: From petroleum tankers to dry cargo, our transport division ensures timely, safe and cost-effective delivery to destinations across Tanzania, Kenya and Zambia, and throughout the wider region.
logistics.logistics.30: Specialised bulk liquid tankers for safe petroleum transport, fully compliant with regional hazmat regulations.
logistics.logistics.32: Heavy-duty flatbed and cargo trucks for construction materials, steel, containers and general freight.
logistics.logistics.34: Dedicated LPG transport vehicles for safe cylinder and bulk gas delivery across East Africa.

lpg.lpg.4: Powering homes, restaurants and industry across 7 East African countries with safe, reliable LPG cylinders.
lpg.lpg.7: Lake Gas Ltd. is East Africa's trusted LPG bottling and distribution company, operating across 7 countries. From domestic cooking to large-scale commercial kitchens and industrial applications, Lake Gas provides safe and reliable liquefied petroleum gas.
lpg.lpg.8: Our innovative composite cylinder range, launched in Dar es Salaam, marks a new revolution in cooking gas technology, combining safety with aesthetics.
lpg.lpg.17: 10 Kg Composite Cylinder New

lubricants.lubricants.4: High-quality automotive, industrial and marine lubricants manufactured and distributed across East and Central Africa.
lubricants.lubricants.7: Lake Lubes Ltd. manufactures and distributes a comprehensive range of lubricants serving automotive, industrial and marine sectors across East and Central Africa.
lubricants.lubricants.8: Our lubricants are formulated to meet international standards, providing superior protection and performance even in Africa's most demanding operating environments.
lubricants.lubricants.13: Engine oils, transmission fluids and greases for passenger and commercial vehicles.
lubricants.lubricants.15: Heavy-duty industrial oils for machinery, compressors and manufacturing equipment.
lubricants.lubricants.17: Specialist marine engine oils and gear lubricants for Lake Victoria and coastal vessels.
lubricants.lubricants.19: Oil analysis, technical advice and maintenance support for corporate fleet customers.

media_center.media_center.15: Tanzania's first HS-CR rebar manufacturer, Lake Steel Ltd., has celebrated a major production milestone, 500,000 metric tonnes of high-strength corrosion-resistant rebars since commissioning.

projects.projects.7: From multi-million dollar construction projects to long-haul logistics contracts, Lake Group has the scale, expertise and equipment to deliver.
projects.projects.11: GCCP supplied specialist high-performance ready-mix concrete to support civil works on Tanzania's flagship 2,115 MW Julius Nyerere Hydropower Project at Stiegler's Gorge.
projects.projects.17: Lake Oil and Lake Trans jointly operate one of East Africa's largest petroleum supply chains, distributing fuel to 8 countries with 700+ trucks running 24/7.

projects.projects.23: Tanzania's first HS-CR rebar producer. The fully computerised 25T/hr rolling mill has supplied 100,000+ MT of high-strength corrosion-resistant steel to the construction sector.

steel.steel.4: Tanzania's first producer of HS-CR reinforcement steel bars. Fully computerised rolling mill with 100,000 MT annual capacity.
steel.steel.6: First in Tanzania. Built to Last.
steel.steel.7: Lake Steel Ltd. is the first company in Tanzania to introduce unique High Strength, Corrosion Resistant (HS-CR) reinforcement steel bars, a game-changer for the East African construction industry.
steel.steel.9: Our state-of-the-art, fully computerised and automatic Steel Rolling Mill plant has a production capacity of up to 25 tonnes per hour, translating to an annual capacity of 100,000 metric tonnes of high-quality steel bars.
steel.steel.12: Our HS-CR bars maintain strength at temperatures up to 600°C, compared to 350°C for ordinary rebars, providing critical fire resistance advantages for major construction projects.
steel.steel.32: Billets tested in our lab. Only approved materials proceed.
steel.steel.34: Billets heated to rolling temperature in our furnace.
steel.steel.38: Quality testing to national and international standards before dispatch.

sustainability.sustainability.4: Lake Group's commitment to environmental responsibility, clean energy transition and reducing our operational footprint.
sustainability.sustainability.7: As a major energy and logistics company operating across 8 countries, Lake Group recognises its responsibility to operate in a manner that minimises environmental impact and contributes to sustainable development across Africa.
sustainability.sustainability.8: Our sustainability strategy covers environmental stewardship, clean cooking energy access, land reclamation, waste management and progressive decarbonisation of our logistics fleet.
sustainability.sustainability.9: LPG, a core business of Lake Gas, plays a critical role in East Africa's clean cooking agenda, reducing dependence on charcoal and firewood, improving indoor air quality and reducing deforestation.
sustainability.sustainability.13: Lake Gas supplies LPG to 7 countries, displacing millions of charcoal cookstoves. Every cylinder sold reduces CO₂, indoor air pollution and deforestation pressure.
sustainability.sustainability.15: All petroleum transport operations are governed by strict zero-tolerance spillage protocols. Our tanker fleet is regularly inspected and certified.
sustainability.sustainability.17: GCCP's quarry operations at Lugoba include progressive land rehabilitation, restoring extracted areas to usable agricultural and ecological land.
sustainability.sustainability.19: Ongoing investment in newer, fuel-efficient trucks, reducing per-kilometre emissions across our 700+ vehicle fleet while improving service reliability.
sustainability.sustainability.22: Countries with clean LPG access via Lake Gas
